title: News & Updates
text: 📰 Latest News Posts

title: The 2025 Korean Geography Conference
text: At the 2025 Korean Geography Conference held on June 27–28, our lab’s master’s degree candidate, Hyemin, presented her thesis titled “Regionalization of the Seoul Metropolitan Area Using Graph Transformer-Based Representation Learning and Spatially Constrained Clustering.” Congratulations to her, and we wish her all the best after graduation!

title: Projects
text:
2026






Micro-Degree Winter Professional Development Program  · 2026


Participated in a collaborative winter professional development program jointly organized by the Seoul National University Learning Sciences Institute and the Seoul Metropolitan Office of Education. The program focused on designing and developing interactive dashboards that can be directly implemented in classroom instruction.


Organized by: Seoul National University Learning Sciences Institute (서울대학교 학습과학연구소)  Seoul Metropolitan Office of Education (서울특별시교육청)

title: Projects
text:
2025






The National Atlas of Korea  · 2024–2025


Participated in the development of interactive web-based atlas content for The National Atlas of Korea. Responsible for building interactive thematic maps and user-driven map interaction components, and for preparing national-scale geospatial data for web deployment.


Organized by: National Geographic Information Institute (국토지리정보원)

title: Projects
text:
2024






The National Atlas of Korea  · 2024–2025


Participated in the development of interactive web-based atlas content for The National Atlas of Korea. Responsible for building interactive thematic maps and user-driven map interaction components, and for preparing national-scale geospatial data for web deployment.


Organized by: National Geographic Information Institute (국토지리정보원)

title: SDS Lab Website Completed
text:
SDS Lab website Lanch
The updated website now presents a complete overview of our research in Spatial Data Science, GeoAI, GIS, and Geospatial Analytics, including detailed project descriptions, publications, and team information.
We hope this platform will serve as a clear and professional representation of our research activities and future directions.
